# Changelog

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED -->

Generated from git conventional commits. Grouped by type and version.

## v4.6.0

## v4.6.1

### Added

- `github-actions` skill: CI/CD patterns, debugging, caching, secrets for GitHub Actions
- `github-actions-rust` skill: cross-compilation matrix, cargo caching, release patterns for Rust CI
- `rules/ecc/github-actions.md`: documents all 4 ECC workflows (ci, release, cd, maintenance)
- `pre:edit-write:workflow-branch-guard` hook: blocks workflow file edits on protected branches (main/master/production)

### Added

- **Adversarial challenge for audit commands (BL-083)**: New `audit-challenger` agent (Sonnet) independently re-interrogates codebase after each domain audit, searches web for best practices, produces challenged findings (confirmed/refuted/amended). Disagreements shown to user. Graceful degradation on failure. Always-on across all 10 `/audit-*` commands + audit-orchestrator.

## v4.7.0

### Added

- **Tiered diagnostics via tracing (BL-091)**: Replaced `log`+`env_logger` with `tracing`+`tracing-subscriber` across all crates. 5-level verbosity: -q (errors), default (warnings), -v (info), -vv (debug), -vvv (trace). `ECC_LOG` env var with `RUST_LOG` deprecated fallback. `ecc status` shows workflow state, versions, and component counts. `ecc config set log-level` persists verbosity preference to `~/.ecc/config.toml`. ADR 0031.

## v4.3.1

### Fixed

- Memory writes from worktree sessions (`write_daily`, `write_memory_index`) now resolve to the main repo's `~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/` directory instead of creating a worktree-specific hash (fixes daily memory and memory-index fragmentation in concurrent sessions)

## v4.3.0

### Audit Remediation (24 smells, 8 user stories)

- **Observable CLI operations (US-001)**: Default log level set to `warn` via `env_logger`. 6+ error-discard sites now emit `log::warn!`. `--verbose` flag produces debug output. Failure banners (`Error: <desc>`) on stderr before `exit(1)`. ecc-workflow binary gains `log`+`env_logger` dependencies.
- **Testable workflow commands (US-002)**: Pure logic extracted from `memory_write.rs` (4 format builder functions). Bounded stdin reading (1MB cap with truncation warning). Integration test monolith (2750 lines) split into 10 per-command test files (<400 lines each).
- **Secure notification hooks (US-003)**: `sanitize_osascript` and `sanitize_powershell` functions escape backslashes, quotes, and cap input at 256 chars. 16 adversarial tests including injection, Unicode, and length attacks.
- **Typed error handling (US-004)**: `ClawError`, `MergeError`, `ConfigAppError`, `InstallError`, `PathValidationError` enums replace `Result<T, String>` across ecc-app. CLI maps error variants to operation names + remediation hints.
- **File size compliance (US-007)**: `validate.rs` (1240 lines) split into 8 submodules. `dev.rs` (1065 lines) split into 5 submodules. `install/global.rs` split into 2 submodules. `merge/helpers.rs` tests extracted.
- **Convention consistency (US-006)**: `Completion.phase` typed as `Phase` enum (was `String`). `Concern` enum and `Timestamp` newtype for `WorkflowState`. `Phase::Unknown` variant for forward-compatible deserialization. `is_claude_available` deduplicated. Port trait methods get `///` doc comments.
- **Domain model improvement (US-008)**: `Validatable<E>` and `Transitionable` behavioral traits added to ecc-domain. D score improved from 0.99 to ~0.79. Corrupt `aliases.json` now emits `log::warn!`. Dev switch rollback logs errors.
- **Documentation (US-005)**: `DEPENDENCY-GRAPH.md` rewritten for 9 Rust crates. `glossary.md` updated (27 `.ts` refs replaced). `ARCHITECTURE.md` counts corrected (51 agents, 24 commands, 110 skills). `bounded-contexts.md` adds backlog+workflow. `MODULE-SUMMARIES.md` adds 3 missing crates. `commands-reference.md` adds spec pipeline. Module dependency diagram corrected to 9 nodes.

### ADRs

- **ADR 0027**: ecc-workflow keeps direct I/O (Functional Core, Imperative Shell)
- **ADR 0028**: Domain abstractness via behavioral traits (Validatable, Transitionable)
- **ADR 0029**: thiserror enums per module, anyhow only in binaries

## v4.2.0

### Features

- **Cargo xtask deploy — full local machine setup (BL-087)**: New `xtask/` crate at repo root (10th in workspace) with `cargo xtask deploy` command. Automates: release build of ecc + ecc-workflow, binary installation to ~/.cargo/bin, `ecc install` config sync, shell completion generation, and shell RC file editing with managed block pattern (`# >>> ecc >>>` / `# <<< ecc <<<`). Backup before first RC edit, atomic writes, dry-run mode. Pure string-based rc_block module with 100% test coverage. Shell detection for zsh/bash/fish. ADR 0026.
- **Deterministic spec/design artifact validation (BL-067)**: New `ecc validate spec <path>` and `ecc validate design <path> [--spec <spec-path>]` subcommands for structural validation of pipeline artifacts. `validate spec` extracts AC definitions via regex, checks sequential numbering with no gaps or duplicates. `validate design` parses PC tables (6-column validation, sequential IDs), cross-references ACs from spec for coverage mapping, and performs topological ordering of PCs by file-overlap to flag dependency violations. Structured JSON output. `spec/` domain module created as a top-level module in `ecc-domain`, separate from `config/` (CCP). ADR 0022 (regex over AST) and ADR 0023 (separate spec module).
- **Web-based upgrade audit command (/audit-web) (BL-081)**: New `/audit-web` command that orchestrates a 4-phase pipeline (INVENTORY, LANDSCAPE SCAN, EVALUATE, SYNTHESIZE) to systematically scan the web for upgrade opportunities and output findings in ThoughtWorks-style Technology Radar format (4+1 quadrants: Techniques, Tools, Platforms, Languages & Frameworks, Feature Opportunities). Phase 2 dispatches 8 parallel web-scout subagents (dep-scanner, arch-scout, tool-scout, ecosystem-scout, competitor-scout, user-request-miner, blog-miner, research-scout) with source triangulation (3+ sources per finding). Cost consent gate after Phase 1 prevents surprise token burn. Reports persisted to `docs/audits/web-radar-YYYY-MM-DD.md`. Dedicated `web-scout` orchestrator agent and reusable `web-radar-analyst` search agent. New `web-research-strategy` skill for standardized search patterns. ADR 0020 (independent from /audit-full) and ADR 0021 (4+1 quadrant format).
- **Worktree display segment in statusline (BL-082)**: Statusline now shows `🌳 wt-name (branch)` when running inside a git worktree, replacing the standalone branch segment to avoid duplication. Worktree detection via `git rev-parse --git-dir` vs `--git-common-dir`, shared newline-delimited cache (5s TTL, backward compatible with legacy single-line format), narrow variant for tight terminals, edge case handling (detached HEAD, bare repos, stale entries). 16 Bats tests with disposable git repo isolation. ADR-0018 documents the branch replacement decision.
- **Replace shell hooks with compiled Rust binary (BL-052)**: New `ecc-workflow` crate (8th in workspace) — single binary with subcommand dispatch replacing all 13 `.claude/hooks/*.sh` scripts. WorkflowState aggregate and Phase value object in `ecc-domain` with compile-time transition enforcement. Atomic state.json writes, ECC_WORKFLOW_BYPASS support, structured JSON output, dual invocation (CLI args + stdin JSON). Cross-platform — no POSIX shell or jq dependency. 54 new tests (28 unit + 26 integration). ADR 0019.
- **Serialized merge-to-main with fast verify (BL-065 Sub-Spec D)**: New `ecc-workflow merge` subcommand acquires exclusive merge lock (60s timeout), rebases worktree branch onto main, runs fast verify gate (cargo build+test+clippy), performs git merge --ff-only, cleans up worktree+branch. MergeError enum for typed error paths (timeout→block, conflict→abort+warn, verify fail→warn). Session branch guard rejects non-session branches. ADR 0024 documents concurrent session architecture.
- **Worktree session isolation and lifecycle (BL-065 Sub-Spec C)**: Pipeline commands (`/spec-*`, `/design`, `/implement`) auto-create git worktrees via `EnterWorktree` for session isolation. `WorktreeName` value object in `ecc-domain` with allowlist validation (prevents injection), generate/parse/validate. Memory writes resolve to main repo root via `resolve_repo_root`. New `ecc worktree gc` command removes stale worktrees (PID-dead or >24h), skips active ones. `ExitWorktree` on pipeline completion cleans up automatically.
- **Shared state locking for concurrent sessions (BL-065 Sub-Spec B)**: New `ecc-flock` shared crate (9th in workspace) extracted from ecc-infra — standalone FlockGuard with RAII Drop using `libc::flock`. All ecc-workflow state.json operations (init, transition, toolchain-persist, phase-gate) serialize via `state.lock`. Memory writes locked per-type (action-log, daily, memory-index, work-item). New atomic `ecc-workflow backlog add-entry` subcommand with `backlog.lock`. Multi-process contention tests prove serialization.
- **Lock infrastructure — FileLock port trait + FlockLock adapter (BL-065 Sub-Spec A)**: New `FileLock` port trait in `ecc-ports` with RAII `LockGuard` (Drop releases lock), `acquire()` and `acquire_with_timeout()` methods, `LockError` enum. POSIX `flock(2)` adapter in `ecc-infra` via `libc` crate (`#[cfg(unix)]`). In-memory `InMemoryLock` test double in `ecc-test-support`. Standalone `resolve_repo_root()` for worktree-safe lock path resolution. Foundation for Sub-Specs B/C/D.
- **/commit slash command (BL-063)**: New `/commit` command for git commits with intelligent staging (git status primary, session context enrichment), atomic-commit enforcement with split guidance, conventional commit message generation with directory-based scope inference, build+test pre-flight that blocks on failure (no force-proceed), workflow state awareness (warns during active /implement), and $ARGUMENTS support for initial message override.
- **Numeric quality scores for adversary agents (BL-036)**: spec-adversary and solution-adversary now produce 0-100 scores per dimension alongside PASS/FAIL/CONDITIONAL verdicts. Thresholds: >=70 avg PASS, 50-69 CONDITIONAL, <50 FAIL, any dimension <50 forces CONDITIONAL. Scores persisted in spec and design Phase Summary tables for quality trend analysis.
- **AskUserQuestion preview field for architecture comparisons (BL-037)**: Add `preview` field usage instructions to 8 Markdown files across grill-me skill, /design command, /spec-dev, /spec-fix, /spec-refactor, configure-ecc skill, interface-designer agent, and interviewer agent. Preview renders Mermaid diagrams, code snippets, and file trees in monospace comparison boxes when presenting 2+ visual alternatives. Add AskUserQuestion to design.md allowed-tools (fixes audit SELF-004 asymmetry). 15-line size guardrail per option.
- **Quarterly MCP version audit script (BL-026)**: Shell script (`scripts/audit-mcp-versions.sh`) that checks pinned MCP server versions against npm registry and reports outdated packages. Includes bats test suite (15 tests), quarterly audit runbook (`docs/runbooks/audit-mcp-versions.md`), package name validation for injection prevention, and exit code semantics (0=current, 1=outdated)
- **Deploy poweruser statusline via ecc install (BL-053)**: Upgraded statusline script with full power-user fields (cost, duration, lines, rate limits, tokens), ANSI color-coded context bar, git branch caching (5s TTL with cross-platform support), terminal-width-aware truncation. Added `StatuslineConfig` domain type, `ecc validate statusline` subcommand, and install integration tests
- **Symlink-based instant config switching (BL-058)**: `ecc dev switch dev|default [--dry-run]` with directory-level symlinks for zero-copy config switching between release-installed and local ECC checkout. FileSystem trait extension (`create_symlink`, `read_symlink`, `is_symlink`), InMemoryFileSystem symlink stub, dev_off symlink safety guard, best-effort rollback, path canonicalization, ADR 0016
- **Deterministic workflow state machine gaps (BL-068)**: Add Idle phase to `Phase` enum for proper cycle closure (Done → Idle → Plan). `Phase::is_gated()` domain method centralizes gating logic (Plan/Solution gated, others ungated). Phase-gate uses typed `WorkflowState::from_json()` deserialization — corrupt state warns instead of silently defaulting to "done". Reset archives state.json before writing Idle state (symmetric with init), acquires state lock. Memory write errors surfaced as warnings (not silently swallowed). E2E lifecycle integration test covers full init → done → reset → reinit cycle. ADR 0025.

### Bug Fixes

- **Fix WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove hooks blocking EnterWorktree tool (BL-085)**: Removed `WorktreeCreate` and `WorktreeRemove` delegation hooks from `hooks.json` that caused Claude Code's `EnterWorktree` tool to fail with "no successful output". These were logging-only hooks incorrectly registered as creation delegates. Replaced with `PostToolUse` notification hooks matching `EnterWorktree`/`ExitWorktree` tool names for session logging. Extended `is_legacy_ecc_hook()` to auto-clean stale entries from users' `settings.json` on next `ecc install`. Unblocks BL-065 Sub-Spec C worktree isolation.

### Refactoring

- **Display full artifacts inline in terminal (BL-062)**: `/spec-*`, `/design`, and `/implement` final phases now read persisted artifacts from disk and display the full document body inline in conversation before summary tables. Eliminates need to open files to review specs, designs, or tasks. Consistent "Full Artifact Display" pattern across all 5 commands with fallback for missing paths. "Artifact File Path" reference shown after tables for future access.
- **Auto-commit backlog edits (BL-059)**: `/backlog add`, `promote`, and `archive` now auto-commit changes with conventional commit messages (`docs(backlog): <action> BL-NNN`). Read-only subcommands (`list`, `match`) unaffected.
- **Offload web research to Task subagents (BL-049)**: Phase 3 in /spec-dev, /spec-fix, /spec-refactor now delegates WebSearch to a Task subagent with isolated context. Raw search tokens stay in subagent; main session receives only the condensed Research Summary.
- **Simplify context management (BL-060)**: Removed graceful exit infrastructure (BL-054/BL-055), context checkpoints from /implement and /audit-full, Resumption Pointer from campaign manifest, strategic compact skill. Superseded ADR-0014. Rely on native plan-accept context clear.
- **Unified grill-me universal questioning protocol (BL-061)**: Refactored 3 independent questioning systems (standalone grill-me, spec-pipeline inline rules, backlog-curator ad-hoc questions) into a single universal grill-me protocol with stage-by-stage AskUserQuestion interaction, challenge loops, cross-stage mutation, question cap (25), and hook-based enforcement (grill-me-gate.sh). Canonical stages: Clarity, Assumptions, Edge Cases, Alternatives, Stress Test. Three modes: standalone, spec-mode, backlog-mode. Decomposed spec-pipeline-shared grab-bag. ADR 0017
- **Context-aware doc generation at end of /implement (BL-056)**: Phase 7.5 dispatches two parallel agents (`module-summary-updater`, `diagram-updater`) to capture MODULE-SUMMARIES entries and Mermaid diagrams while session context is fresh. Non-blocking failures, cross-link fixup pass, doc-enforcement hook extension, ADR 0015
- **Grill-me-adversary companion skill (BL-057)**: Opt-in "adversary mode" for grill-me interviews with adaptive adversarial questioning, answer scoring (completeness + specificity 0-3), follow-up probing, question-generation challenge, and three-attempt cap with branch status labels
- **Graceful mid-session exit (BL-055, BL-054)**: Context-aware exit for /implement and /audit-full. Statusline side-channel writes context % to temp file; commands check at breakpoints. Two thresholds: 75% warn, 85% exit, 95% hard ceiling. Audit re-entry skips completed domains.
- Add campaign manifest for amnesiac agents: externalize all pipeline state to disk via campaign.md per work item, toolchain persistence in state.json, draft spec persistence before adversary review, disk fallbacks in design command, scope-check.sh bug fix, implement.md decomposition into 4 sub-skills (BL-035, ADR 0013)
- Add wave-based parallel TDD execution: group independent PCs into waves, dispatch concurrently via worktree-isolated subagents (max 4 per wave), wave-boundary regression verification, git tags for rollback, backward compatible with sequential execution (BL-032, ADR 0012)
- Add explanatory narrative to all 22 ECC commands: "narrate before acting" convention with shared narrative-conventions skill, agent delegation narration, gate failure remediation, and progress communication across pipeline, audit, and utility commands (BL-051, ADR 0011)
- Add interview-me skill and interviewer agent: collaborative requirements interview with 8-stage methodology, codebase-aware questioning, security hard-gate, and structured output to docs/interviews/; enhance ecc validate skills with frontmatter field validation (BL-013, ADR 0010)
- Add comprehensive pipeline output summaries: table-based Phase Summary sections for /spec-*, /design, and /implement commands with grill-me decisions, adversary findings, artifact persistence, and idempotent overwrite; extract shared spec sections into spec-pipeline-shared skill (BL-048, ADR 0009)
- Add design-an-interface skill and interface-designer agent: divergent interface exploration with parallel sub-agents, 5-dimension comparison matrix, convergence detection, and user synthesis (BL-014, ADR 0008)
- Add /catchup session resumption command: structured summary of workflow state, tasks progress, git status, stale detection, and daily memory activity (BL-017)
- Persist tasks.md as session-independent implementation progress tracker alongside spec.md and design.md in `docs/specs/` — tracks PC statuses with timestamp trails, serves as authoritative cross-session resume source (BL-030)
- Automatic session-to-memory persistence: daily files at `memory/daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md` with activity auto-capture on phase transitions and session stop, cross-day context linking (BL-047)
- Add cargo test, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, and cargo audit to CI pipeline — closes critical quality gate gaps
- Fresh context per TDD task: each PC's RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle runs in an isolated tdd-executor subagent with fresh context; parent owns regression verification and Loop Completion (BL-031, ADR 0007)
- Persist specs and designs as versioned file artifacts in docs/specs/ — survive sessions, re-readable on /implement re-entry (BL-029)
- Rename pipeline: /plan → /spec, /solution → /design with doc-first Plan Mode review in all 3 phases (ADR 0006)
- Display open backlog items as selectable options when `/plan` is called without arguments
- Add cross-session memory system: append-only action log + grouped work item files, written by phase-transition hooks (BL-027)
- Add grill-me adversarial interview skill: 5-stage stress test with branch tracking, vocabulary detection, and transcript output (BL-011)
- Add ubiquitous-language domain extraction skill: extracts types, functions, errors into structured glossary per bounded context (BL-010)
- Add mandatory Web Research phase to plan-dev, plan-fix, plan-refactor commands with WebSearch/exa and Research Summary output (BL-028)

### Fixes

- Fix phase-gate hook allowlist to allow spec/plan/design artifact writes during active workflow phases; refactor allowlist into `is_allowed_path` function with single-source-of-truth `ALLOWED_PATHS` array; add 20-case bash test suite (BL-046)
- Replace 10 silent `let _ =` error-swallowing sites with `log::warn!` across ecc-app and ecc-infra
- Fix CLAUDE.md test count (1185, was showing 1190/1180)
- Add CODEOWNERS file and move CONTRIBUTING.md/CHANGELOG.md to repository root
- Enforce atomic one-question-per-turn grill-me interviews via AskUserQuestion in all plan commands
- Enforce Plan Mode and atomic commits in /implement with MUST/NEVER blocking language
- Resolve MD028 blockquote lint errors in solution, implement, plan-dev, plan-fix, plan-refactor, and plan commands

### Refactoring

- Remove 5 alias/superseded command files (plan.md, solution.md, plan-dev.md, plan-fix.md, plan-refactor.md) and update 60+ file references to canonical spec/design pipeline names (BL-045)
- Decompose install/mod.rs (920 lines) into global.rs, init.rs, resolve.rs submodules — mod.rs reduced to 72 lines
- Apply cargo fmt with edition 2024 across 543 files; add rustfmt.toml and .git-blame-ignore-revs
- Add skills preload and negative examples to spec-adversary, solution-adversary, drift-checker, planner agents (BL-006 through BL-009)
- Refactor robert to read-only: remove Write from tools, add memory:project, architecture-review skill, anti-patterns section; /review now writes robert-notes.md (BL-004, BL-005)
- Replace free-text "Wait for user approval" with AskUserQuestion in audit-orchestrator and doc-orchestrator (Tier B native tooling fixes)
- Add context:"fork" to summary-output Task invocations in solution.md and verify.md
- Add TodoWrite progress tracking to build-fix, backlog commands and 14 moderate-complexity agents
- Replace manual git worktree with EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree in ecc-test-mode
- Add allowedTools scoping to sub-agent spawns in architect, architect-module, code-reviewer, arch-reviewer, and requirements-analyst agents (Tier A native tooling fixes)
- Add context:"fork" isolation to parallel spawns in arch-reviewer and requirements-analyst agents
- Add TodoWrite progress tracking to requirements-analyst, doc-validator, doc-analyzer agents and configure-ecc, django-verification, springboot-verification skills
- Add TodoWrite progress tracking to plan-dev, plan-fix, plan-refactor, solution, audit-full, verify, audit-orchestrator, doc-orchestrator, spec-adversary, and solution-adversary
- Add TodoRead re-entry support to solution and implement commands

## v2.0.0

### Features

- Add /audit command for codebase health audits (7 domains: architecture, evolution, testing, security, observability, error handling, conventions)
- Add audit-orchestrator, evolution-analyst, test-auditor, observability-auditor, error-handling-auditor, convention-auditor agents
- Add 5 audit skills (evolutionary-analysis, test-architecture, observability-audit, error-handling-audit, convention-consistency)
- Enhance /doc-suite with plan mode, guidelines, quality gates, README sync, and CLAUDE.md challenge
- Upgrade 9 agents to opus for deep reasoning tasks
- Add User Story persistence with US-RECAP index
- Add stories mode with parallel execution and recap to /plan
- Add requirements-analyst agent for User Story decomposition
- Auto-enter Claude Code plan mode on /plan invocation
- Wire --clean and --clean-all flags into ecc install
- Add clean module for surgical ECC artifact removal
- Add config-audit module for ECC config diffing
- Add agentshieldignore config and CI validation
- Expand verify with code review, arch review, coverage, and dead code scan
- Add refactor/security modes to plan, remove internal code review
- Add source sync and codemaps phases to doc-suite
- Add custom diagram registry for doc-suite regeneration
- Add TDD execution flow to /plan command
- Add TDD-ready plan format to planner agent
- Integrate diagram-generator into doc-suite pipeline
- Extend doc-analyzer with diagram opportunity detection
- Add /doc-diagrams command
- Add diagram-generator agent and diagram-generation skill
- Add ecc-hook and ecc-shell-hook bin entries for self-resolving hooks
- Add doc-coverage-reminder hook
- Add doc-suite and individual doc commands (/doc-analyze, /doc-generate, /doc-validate, /doc-coverage)
- Add doc-orchestrator agent
- Add doc-analyzer, doc-generator, doc-validator, doc-reporter agents
- Add doc-analysis and doc-quality-scoring skills
- Add shared test harness and single-process runner
- Forward --no-interactive flag from install.sh to orchestrator
- Interactive diff review as default install mode
- Add unchanged category to MergeReport
- Add contentsDiffer utility for file comparison

### Fixes

- Remove archived doc sub-commands from validation tests
- Remove tests for archived tdd.md and orchestrate.md commands
- Restore Mandatory Code Review section in plan.md
- Update e2e.md cross-references to active commands
- Detect ECC hooks by package identifier instead of path patterns

- Remove task:completed:notify hook to reduce notification noise — only stop:notify remains
- Archive 41 commands, keep 5 essential (later expanded to 6 with /audit)
- Update rules, examples, and README to reference simplified commands
- Remove orphaned loop-operator agent

- Rename pluginRoot to eccRoot in TypeScript source

### Documentation

- Update CLAUDE.md plan modes to include stories

- Update codemaps for diagram-generator and custom registry
- Regenerate documentation suite after plan-TDD integration

## v1.0.3

### Features

- Prompt to add .claude/settings.local.json to .gitignore

## v1.0.2

### Fixes

- Audit and fix shell completion -- add completion-server, per-shell scripts, built-ins-only postinstall

## v1.0.1

### Features

- Professional npm CLI -- omelette completion, Node entrypoint, health check, types
- Add everything-claude-code instincts for continuous learning
- Add everything-claude-code skill generated by ECC Tools
- Auto-setup shell completion on npm install
- Add Antigravity IDE support via `--target antigravity` flag
- Add shell completion for bash, zsh, and fish
- Add Rust language rules
- Deliver v1.8.0 harness reliability and parity updates
- Add help command with per-command detail

### Fixes

- Make ace-tool MCP optional in multi-* commands with built-in fallbacks

---

## v1.0.0

### Features

- Distribute as npm package @lebocqtitouan/ecc
- Distribute ecc as installable CLI (Homebrew + Linux curl installer)
- Add init subcommand for per-project Claude setup
- One-command full install -- agents, commands, skills, rules, hooks
- Add hexagonal/DDD architect chain and uncle-bob consultant
- Add autonomous-loops and plankton-code-quality skills
- Automatic project type and framework detection
- Project-scoped instinct isolation
- Add exa-web-search to MCP config template
- Separate core vs niche skills and enforce research-first default
- Add generic content and investor skills
- Add chief-of-staff communication triage agent
- Add Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode harnesses
- Add development-workflow.md with Feature Implementation Workflow
- Add security guides and sanitize external links
- Add swift-concurrency-6-2, foundation-models-on-device, liquid-glass-design skills
- Add skill-stocktake, search-first, visa-doc-translate skills
- Auto-detect formatter in post-edit hook (Biome/Prettier)
- Add NanoClaw agent REPL -- persistent session-aware CLI for ECC
- Add docker-patterns skill
- Add swiftui-patterns skill and Swift language-specific rules
- Add TypeScript declaration files for all core libraries
- Add comprehensive Golang language support
- Add /sessions command for session history management
- Add Python/Django support and Java Spring Boot skills
- Unify commands and skills (v1.2.0)
- Add comprehensive CI/CD pipeline
- Cross-platform support with Node.js scripts
- Package as Claude Code plugin with marketplace distribution
- Add strategic-compact hook, memory persistence hooks, context files
- Add continuous learning and strategic compact skills

### Fixes

- Calendar-accurate date validation in parseSessionFilename
- Stdin overflow bug in hook scripts -- truncate chunks within MAX_STDIN limit
- Harden utils.js edge cases and add input validation
- Path traversal in install.sh, error logging in hooks
- Windows compatibility for hook scripts (execFileSync + tmux)
- Eliminate child process spawns during session startup
- Prevent command injection in Prettier hook
- Resolve multiple reported issues (#205, #182, #188, #172, #173)

### Refactoring

- Split Feature Implementation Workflow from git-workflow.md
- Slim down 4 remaining oversized agents (73% reduction)
- Move embedded patterns from agents to skills
- Extract inline PostToolUse hooks into external scripts
- Restructure rules into common + language-specific directories

### Documentation

- Add token optimization guide
- Add hooks guide, Django example, Spring Boot skills
- Add Chinese (zh-CN, zh-TW), Japanese translations
- Add The Longform Guide and The Shortform Guide

### Tests

- 1260 total tests across all modules
- Edge-case coverage for session-manager, session-aliases, hooks, validators
- Cross-platform Windows/macOS/Linux compatibility tests
